content: We can model naturally occurring biological pathogenic agents (from the biology/chemistry definition of Topic B) as active, distributed information-gathering agents that rely on a scalable, shared memory architecture to adapt their evolutionary strategies across heterogeneous host populations. This frames the cumulative mutational records of viral or bacterial outbreaks across hosts as a form of multi-agent active learning, where each pathogen is an individual agent and shared genomic mutation databases serve as the collective retained memory.
source_topics: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
trigger_topic: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
